headline: Persistent memory systems for AI agents surged as Microsoft Research and an open protocol pushed unified context stores.
body: Memory was the day's clearest rotation: Microsoft Research introduced Memora, which separates storage from retrieval and uses a harmonic representation to balance abstraction and specificity, while the Open Memory Protocol proposed a single memory store interface spanning Claude, ChatGPT, and Curso, with episodic memory breaking out alongside both. Scaling moved in parallel as LongCat-2.0 arrived as a 1.6T-parameter Mixture of Experts model with 48B active parameters. Startups continued chasing model defensibility, with Wix-owned Base44 rolling out its own model to eventually outperform frontier systems, and Google made Gemini's personalized image generation free for US users. Meta drew scrutiny for posing as teenagers to probe rival chatbots with harmful prompts, raising adversarial-robustness and safety questions.
